economic_notes: Investments held in Kakina Economic Development Group LP (10% interest), Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership, Sovereign Wealth LP (holds Hydro One shares, 0.3918% interest), Treaty 3 Investment Group, and Niiwin Partnership (Four Winds Partnership between Shoal Lake 40, Obashkaandagaang, Niishaachewan and Wauzhushk Onigum). $400,000 invested in Niiwin Partnership during 2023.
notes: Consolidated entity includes Washagamis Bay First Nation and Washagamis Bay Investment Corporation. Washagamis Bay Investment Corporation has $894,000 owing to Indigenous Services Canada related to arrears in land lease payments 2004-2023, offset by a receivable from Ottawa Trust Fund. Capital asset purchases of $3,397,532 during the year with no capital asset contributions received (vs $3,973,261 received in 2022). Net financial debt increased significantly from ($799,196) to ($3,778,734). Prior year accumulated surplus restated — beginning of 2022 was $17,061,960 per 2023 statement but 2022 closing was $20,151,519 including $3,973,261 capital assets contribution. OSR estimate includes rental income, miscellaneous, cottage lot fees and partnership distributions. Total government transfers estimated from ISC, MCSS, FNIH, Education, MIA, MHLTC, CMHC, Kenora Chiefs Advisory, Grand Council Treaty 3, Bimose Tribal Council, Shooniyaa Wa-Biitong lines.
